# Rimsky

> Project-agnostic reactive node-graph orchestration platform. Workflows are graphs of nodes; an `invalidate` propagates through the graph and the scheduler recalculates eligible stale nodes. Coding agents are the primary documentation consumer.

## Concepts

The canonical concept catalog lives in the rimsky source repo at [`.ok-planner/design/concepts.md`](https://github.com/fallguyconsulting/rimsky/blob/main/.ok-planner/design/concepts.md) — an auto-generated TOC over the per-concept files in [`.ok-planner/design/concepts/`](https://github.com/fallguyconsulting/rimsky/tree/main/.ok-planner/design/concepts). Each file carries a definition, purpose, boundaries, and invariants, plus a Notes section recording how the concept evolved. Inline `@concept: <slug>` annotations in the source code point at enforcement sites for each concept; `rg '@concept: <slug>'` finds them.

Read the TOC first to know what nouns the project traffics in, then read the per-concept files for the specific ones load-bearing for your task.

## Protocols

- [ClaimProducer](protocols/claim-producer.md): How to implement a claim producer (Open / Commit / Abandon / Release / Capabilities).
- [Executor](protocols/executor.md): How to implement an executor — `Executor.Execute` (required dispatch protocol) and the optional `ExecutorObservability` protocol (Capabilities / GetTrace / StreamTrace).
- [LifecycleSubscriber](protocols/lifecycle-subscriber.md): How to implement a lifecycle subscriber (six template/instance state-transition hooks).

## Examples

- [Minimal rimsky.yml](agents/examples/minimal-rimsky-yml.md)
- [Minimal template and instance](agents/examples/minimal-template-and-instance.md)
- [Two-node template with a claim dependency](agents/examples/two-node-with-claim.md)
- [Template demonstrating attribute-default inertness](agents/examples/claude-agent-attribute-defaults.md)
- [Holding-subgraph template demonstrating held-claim resolution](agents/examples/holding-subgraph.md)
- [rimsky-compose multi-template project](agents/examples/rimsky-compose-multi-template.md)
- [Atomic staging via a held subgraph](agents/examples/atomic-staging.md)
